summary: Verified Fork·Fate keyless (GOOGLE_API_KEY unset) build - expanded 23-restaurant seed, ZIP now optional, curated fallback treated as primary path. 16/16 pytest backend tests pass. Full frontend E2E (blank-zip spin, invalid-zip toast, respin, reset, cuisine+price filter) all pass. No 'demo/unavailable' messaging shown.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend CUISINES list in Home.jsx still includes 'Sushi' which is NOT in the backend seed (backend uses 'Japanese' for sushi spots). Selecting the Sushi chip would return 0 results and show a 'no spots match' toast - minor UX issue since user might expect it to work.
Backend google_places_search still uses locationBias radius of 50000m (~31 miles) while MAX_RADIUS_MILES=50 (~80km). Only matters if a real GOOGLE_API_KEY is added later.
Backend uses @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI (lifespan handlers preferred). Non-blocking.
seed_data_creation: None. Mongo seeded 23 restaurants on backend startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GOOGLE_API_KEY unset by design. /api/places/search returns source='curated' with 23 items sorted by distance. Request body accepts zip_code absent, null, or 5-digit string. Frontend chip testids: cuisine-filter-italian, cuisine-filter-mexican, etc. Note: 'Sushi' chip exists in frontend but not in seed cuisines. Price chips: use text selector button:has-text('$$'). Sonner toast used for validation errors.
